items.q: Which particle has a negative charge?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Electron. “Proton” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Where is the mass of an atom mainly found?
items.why: It's In the nucleus. “In the electron shells” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: If a neutral atom has 8 protons, how many electrons does it have?
items.why: Exact answer: 8. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
